history.hypothesis: SPY exhibits momentum tendencies that can be captured by combining rate of change with EMA trend filtering. Volume confirmation reduces false signals in choppy markets, while ATR-based trailing stops protect against drawdowns during reversals. This approach should achieve Sharpe >1.5 with controlled drawdown on SPY's smoother trend patterns.

The ROC-based momentum strategy fails due to regime fragility — it enters on breakouts that get whipsawed in sideways/choppy markets (2024: -0.75, 2026: -1.46). Switching to a pullback-based approach using RSI regime filtering should be more robust: (1) Long RSI as regime filter prevents entries in bearish/sideways markets, (2) Short RSI dip recovery catches pullbacks within established uptrends rather than chasing breakouts, (3) Volume confirmation ensures genuine institutional buying interest. This pullback-style entry naturally adapts across regimes because it only trades when both the trend is established (regime filter) AND a dip occurs (entry timing), avoiding the whipsaw problem entirely.
